Without a doubt, the Chase Ultimate Rewards program is one of the most popular travel rewards programs among points and miles aficionados — and for good reason.

With tons of ways to earn Ultimate Rewards points, the program may be the best credit card rewards program currently available, and it should be a top priority for anyone who wants to book award flights.

Once you have tackled earning some points, you can redeem Ultimate Rewards for award flights or hotel award stays across the world.

Let’s dig into what the Ultimate Rewards program has to offer so you can start putting together your next amazing vacation!

Advertisement

## What Is Chase Ultimate Rewards?

Chase Ultimate Rewards is a credit card rewards program that allows cardholders to earn points on every purchase.

To [earn Ultimate Rewards](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/how-to-earn-chase-ultimate-rewards-points/), you must hold an affiliated Chase credit card. Once you are approved for a Chase card that earns Ultimate Rewards, you will be enrolled in the program automatically.

The number of points you earn on purchases depends on which card you use and under what category the purchase will code. Each Ultimate Rewards card has bonus categories that allow you to earn between 2x and 10x points per dollar spent on the card.

These flexible, and therefore valuable, points can be [redeemed in a variety of ways](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/best-ways-to-redeem-chase-ultimate-rewards-points/), but the best redemption option, by far, is for travel. Other redemption options, such as merchandise, direct statement credits, or gift cards, could result in far less value for your points.

With the right combination of Chase cards, you can earn tons of Ultimate Rewards points and have flexible redemption options.

## Which Credit Cards Earn Ultimate Rewards Points?

![Virgin Atlantic Upper Class Seat2](https://upgradedpoints.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/09/Virgin-Atlantic-Upper-Class-Seat2.jpg?auto=webp&disable=upscale&width=1200)Ultimate Rewards points can be redeemed via Chase’s travel portal for flights and hotels, or transferred to travel partners. Image Credit: Virgin Atlantic

With each Chase card earning differently based on its own bonus categories, the key to earning the most Ultimate Rewards points possible is selecting the [combination of cards](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/best-credit-card-combinations/) that ensures every purchase you make will earn rewards.

The good news is that you can combine Ultimate Rewards points across all Ultimate Rewards-earning cards to maximize both earning and redemption options.

We’ll talk more about redemption options shortly, but these options also vary by card. For example, you must hold at least one premium Ultimate Rewards card to expand your redemption options and be able to [transfer your Chase points to airline and hotel partners](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/chase-ultimate-rewards-transfer-partners/).

With a collection of Ultimate Rewards earning cards for nearly every consumer, you’re sure to find a match for your spending mix, redemption options that fit your travel objectives, and a card that matches your comfort zone for paying an annual fee.

Let’s get started discussing Ultimate Rewards points and the best cards for earning and redeeming these valuable rewards.

**Hot Tip:** If you’re ready to get started and earn a ton of points quickly, check out [how to earn 100k Chase points in 90 days](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/earn-100k-chase-ultimate-rewards-points/)!

Additionally, it offers flexible point redemption options, no foreign transaction fees, and **excellent travel insurance coverage including primary car rental insurance**. With benefits like these, it’s easy to see why this card is an excellent choice for any traveler.

The Chase Sapphire Preferred card is one of the most popular Chase cards due to its generous [welcome bonus](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/sign-up-bonuses/) offer and elevated earning on travel, dining, and Lyft purchases.

Holding this card also allows you to redeem your Ultimate Rewards for increased value on the [Chase travel portal](https://upgradedpoints.com/travel/chase-ultimate-rewards-travel-portal/) and the additional option of transferring points to travel partners to receive potentially greater value.

While the card comes with a lower annual fee than most premium travel rewards cards, the Chase Sapphire Preferred card does not fall short on travel and shopping benefits and protections. In fact, the card has some of the [best travel insurance available](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/reviews/chase-sapphire-preferred-card/travel-insurance-benefits/).

**Hot Tip:** Each Chase card that earns Ultimate Rewards comes with the opportunity to earn an introductory welcome bonus after meeting minimum spending requirements, normally in the first 3 months after card approval. For example, you may be able to get a [75,000-100,000 welcome bonus offer for the Chase Sapphire Preferred Card](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/reviews/chase-sapphire-preferred-card/70k-80k-bonus-offer/).

Rewards Center

[Chase Ultimate Rewards](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/chase-ultimate-rewards-review/)

In addition to elevated earning on Chase travel, dining, and at drugstores, the Freedom Flex card is known for its [quarterly bonus categories](https://upgradedpoints.com/news/chase-freedom-5x-categories/) that allow you to earn **5% cash-back** per dollar spent on up to $1,500 in purchases each quarter.

Quarterly bonus categories change each quarter and have included merchants and services such as supermarkets/grocery stores, transportation, gas stations, warehouse clubs, online shopping, PayPal, restaurants, and more.

The card earns rewards, initially, in the form of Ultimate Rewards points. Those points can then be redeemed for cash-back, travel, shopping, and gift cards, but the true value of the points can be realized when combined with those from one of the premium Ultimate Rewards cards (i.e. one with an annual fee).

## How To Earn Chase Ultimate Rewards

### Welcome Bonuses and Bonus Earning Categories

The primary way you’ll earn Ultimate Rewards points is by qualifying for the initial [welcome bonus offered](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/sign-up-bonuses/) immediately after you’re approved for a card, then subsequently with ongoing spending on the card.

You’ll want to take notice of the card’s bonus categories and be sure to use your card for spending in those elevated earning categories.

There are, however, additional ways to earn Ultimate Rewards points. Here’s a list of additional Ultimate Rewards earning opportunities.

### Shop Through Chase

![Shop With Chase](https://upgradedpoints.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/08/Shop-With-Chase.jpg?auto=webp&disable=upscale&width=1200)Earn up to 25x points by shopping via the Shop through Chase shopping portal. Image Credit: Chase

Earn unlimited additional Ultimate Rewards points per dollar spent by shopping at the **Shop through Chase** online mall.

The bonus Ultimate Rewards points you earn are on top of any bonus categories on one of the Chase Ultimate Rewards cards.

Be aware that you **must use the link provided in the shopping portal** and make your purchases during the same visit in order to receive these points. There are also restrictions on using coupons, discounts, and other offers for your purchases.

With over 240 stores from which to select, you’ll find offers that can range (and fluctuate) from 1 additional Ultimate Rewards point per dollar spent at retailers such as Best Buy, up to an additional 25 points per dollar spent at magazines.com or 20x at restaurants.com.

For step-by-step instructions and tips for using the [Chase shopping portal](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/chase-shopping-portal/), we’ve put together all the information you need in our article on the topic.

### Book Travel Through the Chase Travel Portal

![Book Chase Travel](https://upgradedpoints.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/08/Book-Chase-Travel.jpg?auto=webp&disable=upscale&width=1200)Book via the Chase travel portal and earn bonus rewards when using your card. Image Credit: Chase

Use the Chase travel portal and earn rewards when you book **flights**, **hotels**, **cars**, **cruises, vacation rentals,** **and activities with your Chase card.**

Booking travel-related activities through [Chase Travel](https://upgradedpoints.com/travel/chase-ultimate-rewards-travel-portal/) ensures you’ll be earning rewards on every travel purchase.

This means you’ll earn 5 **x points per dollar spent** on the Chase Sapphire Preferred card. You’ll earn **8x points** when using the [Chase Sapphire Reserve card](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/reviews/chase-sapphire-reserve/) for rental cars, flights, or hotels. The [Ink Business Preferred card](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/reviews/chase-ink-business-preferred-credit-card/), meanwhile, earns **3x points** on travel.

One advantage of booking through the portal is that your airline loyalty program information can be stored in the system and you’ll automatically earn miles when you book. Practically speaking, Chase buys the ticket on your behalf.

Keep in mind, if you book a hotel stay through the Chase travel portal, it’s unlikely your elite status will be recognized by the hotel and you are unlikely to earn hotel points for your stay.

### Earn Points With Chase Refer-A-Friend

![The Chase Sapphire Referral Page](https://upgradedpoints.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/03/Chase_Sapphire_Referral_Example-e1628615314202.png?auto=webp&disable=upscale&width=1200)Earn thousands of points when referring friends and family for Chase cards. Image Credit: Chase

We all like to share things we like with our friends, but it’s even better to get a reward for it!

When you [refer your friends and they’re approved for a Chase card](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/chase-refer-a-friend/), you can earn tens of thousands of points.

To find your referral link, go to Chase’s Refer-A-Friend [page](https://www.chase.com/referafriend/catch-all). Just input your details to see if your card has available referrals offers. Note that each of your cards must be input separately but your referral will be able to select from available Chase cards.

If you have difficulty with the referral link, you can call Customer Service for assistance.

**Hot Tip:** Send your friends the referral link via email and get started to earning your bonus Ultimate Rewards points!

## How To Redeem Ultimate Rewards Points

Redemption options and the value you receive for your Ultimate Rewards points will be determined by which card you use to redeem your points, either travel rewards cards or cash-back cards.

1. **Travel rewards cards** include the Chase Sapphire Preferred card, Ink Business Preferred Card, and Chase Sapphire Reserve card
2. **Cash-back cards include the** Freedom Flex card, Freedom Unlimited card, Ink Business Cash Card, and Ink Business Unlimited card

Travel rewards cards offer more redemption options, including the ability to transfer points to the [Chase travel partners](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/chase-ultimate-rewards-transfer-partners/) and redeem for travel via the Chase travel portal.

We’ll discuss all the ways you can utilize your rewards, but if you’re looking for specific travel redemption options, check out the [best ways to redeem Chase points for travel](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/best-ways-to-redeem-chase-ultimate-rewards-points/)!

### Chase Ultimate Rewards Transfer Partners

![Chase Portal Transfer](https://upgradedpoints.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/08/Chase-Portal-Transfer.jpg?auto=webp&disable=upscale&width=1200)Receive potentially more value for your rewards by transferring to travel partners. Image Credit: Chase

Transferring points to airline and hotel partners will often be the best use of your points, but this redemption is only available with the Chase premium **travel rewards cards** listed above.

To transfer points, log in at [chase.com/ultimaterewards](http://chase.com/ultimaterewards) and select **Transfer to Travel Partners** from the drop-down menu at the top of the home page.

You’ll then be able to select the loyalty program and the number of points you want to transfer. You will need your loyalty program sign-in information to complete the transfer.

The information in your travel profile must match the personal information in your desired loyalty account. In most cases, you can transfer points to accounts of [authorized users](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/reviews/american-express-platinum-card/adding-authorized-users/) on your card and members of your household.

Most transfers are completed almost instantly or within 24 to 48 hours but may take as long as 6 to 8 weeks. Minimum transfer amounts also apply.

Once completed, points cannot be transferred back into your Chase account, so make sure you want to transfer the points!

**Hot Tip:** You don’t need a HUGE chunk of points in order to take advantage of your Chase Ultimate Rewards… check out our guide to the [best ways to use 10k (or fewer) Chase Ultimate Rewards points](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/use-10000-chase-ultimate-rewards/) for some ideas!

When you sign in to [transfer points](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/chase-ultimate-rewards-transfer-partners/), you’ll be able to select your preferred airline from the transfer partner list.

**Bottom Line:** Airline and hotel transfer partners can provide some of the best value for your Ultimate Rewards points. Be sure to check for award availability in your desired loyalty program prior to transferring points, however, as the process cannot be reversed.

### Redeem Ultimate Rewards via the Chase Travel Portal

If you have the Ink Business Preferred card or Chase Sapphire Preferred card, your Ultimate Rewards points are worth up to 1.75 cents each when booking eligible premium cabin tickets on select airlines via Points Boost (through the [Chase travel portal](https://upgradedpoints.com/travel/chase-ultimate-rewards-travel-portal/)).

The Chase Sapphire Reserve card provides the best deal through the travel portal as each point is worth up to 2 cents each through Points Boost.

When booking an airline ticket or hotel stay, **make sure to compare the value** of transferring points to travel partners versus booking through the travel portal. Sometimes, the travel portal will actually be the better way to go.

If you have a no-annual-fee Chase Ultimate Rewards-earning card, you can still redeem points via the Chase travel portal, but points will be worth just 1 cent each.

You can purchase travel via the portal, use points, or use a combination of points and cash. Either way, the purchase will be treated as a cash transaction by the airline and you will be eligible to earn the associated amount of frequent flyer miles.

For more details on getting the most value for your travel purchases via the Chase travel portal, check out our step-by-step tutorial.

### Redeem for Chase Experiences

![Chase Experiences homepage](https://upgradedpoints.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/08/Chase-Experiences-homepage.jpg?auto=webp&disable=upscale&width=1200)You’ll find a variety of unique and potentially valuable redemption options with exclusive Chase Experiences. Image Credit: Chase

If you’re looking for alternative ways to use your points, you might try booking experiences. Chase curates [exclusive experiences](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/best-credit-card-rewards-programs-for-booking-experiences/) that you would likely not be able to access on your own.

Check back often for exclusive offers and redemption values as they are updated on a regular basis.

You can pay for these offers with cash or points at a value of 1 cent per point.

The [Chase Experiences portal](http://experience.chase.com/) is separate from the Chase portal, so you may have to log back into your Chase account after visiting the site.

### Cash-Back as Statement Credit or Direct Deposit

![Redeem Ultimate Rewards Cash](https://upgradedpoints.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/08/Redeem-Ultimate-Rewards-Cash.jpg?auto=webp&disable=upscale&width=1200)Ultimate Rewards points can be redeemed for cash back at a value of 1 cent per point. Image Credit: Chase

Chase lets you use your points as credits on your statement at 1 cent per point. Simply transfer your cash-back earnings to your balance as a statement credit.

To redeem for statement credit, click the **Use Points** option on the Ultimate Rewards home screen, and then click **Get Cash Back**. The minimum redemption amount is $20.00, or 2,000 points. You’ll also have the option on this page to cash out points and transfer funds to your bank account.

Another option for a statement credit is to use [Pay Yourself Back](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/chase-pay-yourself-back-is-it-worth-it/) which reimburses you for specific purchases.

### Gift Cards

![Redeem Ultimate Rewards Gift Cards](https://upgradedpoints.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/08/Redeem-Ultimate-Rewards-Gift-Cards.jpg?auto=webp&disable=upscale&width=1200)If you must redeem your points for gift cards, always wait for a sale. Image Credit: Chase

You can redeem your Ultimate Rewards points for gift cards for merchants where you normally shop such as iTunes, Lowe’s, and many more.

Most options are valued at 1 cent per point, however, some gift cards can offer a better value with 10% or more in discounts.

Redeeming for gift cards may not be the best value for your Ultimate Rewards points.

### Shopping via Amazon.com or Apple Purchases

![Ultimate Rewards Amazon and Apple](https://upgradedpoints.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/08/Ultimate-Rewards-Amazon-and-Apple.jpg?auto=webp&disable=upscale&width=1200)Redeem Ultimate Rewards at Amazon or for Apple purchase via the Chase portal. Image Credit: Chase

Chase Ultimate Rewards partners directly with Amazon.com for merchandise redemptions. This means you can shop directly on Amazon’s website, rather than go through the Chase shopping portal.

Your Ultimate Rewards points will only receive a value of 8/10 of a cent per point, however, so **it is not a good-value redemption option**. You can also combine your points with cash or other payment methods.

Link your Chase and Amazon accounts at any time to use this redemption option, including at the time of checkout.

You cannot use Ultimate Rewards directly at the Apple store but you can redeem Ultimate Rewards for **Apple purchases via the Chase portal** at a value of 1 cent each.

**Bottom Line:** Not all redemption options are created equal. Your best value may be realized when redeeming Ultimate Rewards points for travel either by transferring points to airline and hotel partners or potentially via the Chase travel portal.

## Using Your Ultimate Rewards Dashboard Portal

Your Chase Ultimate Rewards portal allows you to view and manage your points, transfer points between cards, check your category spending, book travel, shop, and redeem rewards.

### Navigating the Ultimate Rewards Portal

To manage your account, simply sign in on the home page of [chase.com/ultimaterewards](http://chase.com/ultimaterewards).

![Ultimate Rewards Home Page](https://upgradedpoints.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/08/Ultimate-Rewards-Home-Page.jpg?auto=webp&disable=upscale&width=1200)The Chase Ultimate Rewards portal is the place to start to manage your Ultimate Rewards account. Image Credit: Chase

Once you log in, select the card you want to view and you’ll be directed to the home page for that card.

![Ultimate Rewards Select Card](https://upgradedpoints.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/08/Ultimate-Rewards-Select-Card.jpg?auto=webp&disable=upscale&width=1200)Use the dropdown screen to switch between card accounts you’d like to view. Image Credit: Chase

#### View Your Points Summaries and Category Spend

The home page screen of any of your credit cards lets you view your earned and available points, your transaction details, and which points will appear on your next statement.

With Chase Ultimate Rewards, it’s easy to check the bonus category spend on Chase credit cards. Plus you’ll have access to all of your current and historical spending data for each card.

![Ultimate Rewards Dashboard Earnings](https://upgradedpoints.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/08/Ultimate-Rewards-Dashboard-Earnings.jpg?auto=webp&disable=upscale&width=1200)You’ll find a separate dashboard for each Chase card, making it easy to manage your Ultimate Rewards. Image Credit: Chase

#### Redeem Your Points

You’ll also be able to select options to make redemptions such as shopping, gift card options, or experiences.

![Ultimate Rewards Dashboard Redeem](https://upgradedpoints.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/08/Ultimate-Rewards-Dashboard-Redeem.jpg?auto=webp&disable=upscale&width=1200)Your Ultimate Rewards dashboard will show you all the available redemption options. Image Credit: Chase

#### Combining Your Points Onto 1 Card

![Ultimate Rewards Portal Combine Points](https://upgradedpoints.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/08/Ultimate-Rewards-Portal-Combine-Points.jpg?auto=webp&disable=upscale&width=1200)The Ultimate Rewards portal is your source for viewing, managing, and redeeming your rewards. Image Credit: Chase

From this main screen, you can also combine all of your points onto 1 card. Select **Combine Points** from the menu at the top of the page and you will be guided through the process.

Advertisement

**Bottom Line:** The Chase Ultimate Rewards portal is your source for viewing, managing, tracking, and learning how to earn and redeem Ultimate Rewards points. Each Chase card has its own separate section within the portal making it simple to manage each individual card account.

## Everything Else You Need To Know

### What Are Chase Ultimate Rewards Points Worth?

The [value of your Ultimate Rewards points](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/chase-ultimate-rewards-points-faqs/) will vary depending on the redemption option you select. As a baseline, however, Chase follows the industry standard with each point worth 1 cent for a basic redemption.

There are ways to secure more value for your points.

When you use your cards to redeem travel rewards via the Chase travel portal, the value of each point can increase when using a Chase premium card.

When you transfer points to any Chase reward travel partners, the redemption value of each point can be worth even more with some redemptions resulting in up to 5 to 8 cents per point for international first and business class options. We value Ultimate Rewards points at an average of 2 cents each.

### Ultimate Rewards Points Expiration

Unlike other rewards programs that cancel your points if your accounts are _**inactive**_ for 6 to 18 months, your Chase rewards points _**never**_ expire, as long as your accounts are open and in good standing.

Once you cancel your account, the points are forfeited. However, you can product change your premium Chase card to a no-annual-fee Chase card and keep your points available.

## Comparing Ultimate Rewards to Other Transferable Points Programs

It’s pretty clear you’ll have access to great travel experiences by earning and redeeming Ultimate Rewards points, but how do these rewards compare to other transferable points programs?

Here’s just a snapshot of additional transferrable rewards programs. Click on the included links for additional information on maximizing each of these travel rewards currencies.

### Ultimate Rewards vs. Amex Membership Rewards

Both programs have their pluses and minuses. For example, more American Express-issued cards earn [Membership Rewards points](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/how-to-earn-american-express-membership-rewards-points/) than Chase-issued cards that earn Ultimate Rewards.

Membership Rewards also has more travel partners than Ultimate Rewards. However, both programs can cover most airlines and awards through the various partner alliances. Chase also has the advantage of a partnership with the [World of Hyatt program](https://upgradedpoints.com/travel/hotels/the-world-of-hyatt-loyalty-program/).

Both Chase and American Express have shopping incentives that allow you to earn more rewards or receive statement credits for shopping at various merchants. Chase offers a shopping portal and [Chase Offers](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/chase-offers/) while American Express has [Amex Offers](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/amex-offers/).

You will be charged a small fee of $0.0006 per point (maximum of $99 per transaction) to [transfer Membership Rewards](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/amex-membership-rewards-transfer-partners/) to U.S. carriers. There are no additional fees to transfer to international carriers. Chase does not charge a fee to transfer Ultimate Rewards to travel partners.

At redemption time there are [tons of ways to redeem both Membership Rewards](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/best-ways-to-redeem-amex-membership-rewards-points/) and [Ultimate Rewards points](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/best-ways-to-redeem-chase-ultimate-rewards-points/) for some fun travel.

### Ultimate Rewards vs. Capital One Miles

Capital One miles can be redeemed directly through the card’s travel portal. For this reason, Capital One miles could be more flexible as you can find the **best deal from any travel provider** and use your miles to receive a statement credit for the travel purchase.

However, you’ll be redeeming miles for 1 cent each versus up to 1.75 to 2 cents each via the Chase portal with Chase premium cards.

Both programs allow you to transfer rewards to airline partners for greater potential value although Capital One ratios for doing so can be a lesser value than Chase’s 1:1. When it comes to hotels, Capital One has Accor and Wyndham as partners while Chase doesn’t. Chase’s best hotel partner is arguably Hyatt.

You can receive good bottom-line value with either program but to learn more about [the best way to earn, redeem, and transfer Capital One miles](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/capital-one-miles-program-review/), here’s an excellent overview.

### Ultimate Rewards vs. Marriott Bonvoy

While Chase provides several cards that earn Ultimate Rewards, you can [earn Marriott Bonvoy points](https://upgradedpoints.com/travel/hotels/how-to-earn-marriott-rewards-points/) with [Marriott Bonvoy-branded cards](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/best-marriott-bonvoy-credit-cards/) issued by both American Express and Chase. Transfers from Ultimate Rewards process almost instantly to Marriott Bonvoy in many cases, while [transferring Marriott Bonvoy points](https://upgradedpoints.com/travel/hotels/marriott-transfer-partners/) can take much longer.

There are some good-value options for redeeming Marriott Bonvoy points for some [amazing flights](https://upgradedpoints.com/travel/hotels/best-ways-to-redeem-marriott-points/) with dozens of airline transfer partners and for [hotel stays](https://upgradedpoints.com/travel/hotels/best-ways-to-redeem-marriott-points-for-hotel-stays/).

### Ultimate Rewards vs. Citi ThankYou Rewards

Finally, we have [Citi ThankYou Rewards](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/citi-thankyou-rewards-review/). You will find several options for [redeeming ThankYou Points](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/best-ways-to-redeem-citi-thankyou-points/) for some awesome travel experiences.

Citi cards that [earn ThankYou Points](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/how-to-earn-citi-thankyou-points/) are eligible for [transfers to the full suite of Citi travel partners](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/citi-thankyou-points-transfer-partners/), although the transfer ratios vary between premium and non-premium cards. With a premium card like the [Citi Strata Premier℠ Card](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/reviews/citi-strata-premier-card/), cardholders can maximize transfers to Citi travel partners by receiving the best ratios.

**Bottom Line:** We estimate the overall relative value, based on earning and redemption options, of Ultimate Rewards points at 2 cents each, Membership Rewards points 2.2 cents each, Capital One miles at 1.8 cents, Marriott Bonvoy points at 0.7 cents each, and ThankYou Points at 1.6 cents each.

## Final Thoughts

When applying for Chase cards, keep in mind [the 5/24 rule](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/chase-5-24-rule/). If you have opened 5 or more cards from any card issuer, including Chase, (not including most business cards) in the last 24 months, you will not be approved for a Chase card.

For that reason, you want to select the best Chase Ultimate Rewards earning cards when you are just starting out. Ultimate Rewards earning cards allow you to accumulate points that can be used to fly with dozens of different airlines thanks to its airline transfer partners. On top of that, you can transfer to Hyatt for some [amazing Hyatt redemptions](https://upgradedpoints.com/travel/hotels/best-ways-to-redeem-hyatt-points/).

With several cards that can help you earn points quickly and some amazing redemption options, Chase Ultimate Rewards-earning cards deserve a place in every traveler’s wallet.

A full overview of [what Chase has to offer](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/chase/) is available for those deciding where to start.
